rationale: The cited paper is used in the self-focus theory section as evidence that self-focused attention increases self-referencing in choice. That proposition is central to the citing paper's H3 logic, where self-focus is the key moderator of political-identity-consistent SEP preferences. Although the citation appears in a grouped set, it supports a specific conceptual claim that the paper then tests across multiple studies, so its role is more than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Goukens et al. (2009) as prior support that private/self-focused attention shifts decisions toward self-referential processing. It uses this as part of the conceptual foundation for predicting that higher self-focus amplifies identity-consistent preferences in SEP choice.
